objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 future words, 'going to' grammar for plans and intentions, reading, speaking, listening, and homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 ирээдүйн үг, going to дүрэм, унших, ярих, сонсох, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Learn how to use 'going to' to talk about future plans, intentions and dreams.
contentMn: Going to ашиглан ирээдүйн төлөвлөгөө, мөрөөдлөө ярьж сурна.

vocabulary.example: I have a plan for next week.
My dream is to travel the world.
I hope to visit Europe.
I want to be a doctor.
I am going to travel next summer.
She is going to visit Japan.
I am going to study hard.
I am going to rest next week.
She starts next month.
He graduates next year.
I am going to call you soon.
I am going to do it later.
I am going to study tomorrow.
I want to go to university.
What career do you want?
I want to be famous one day.
I want to be successful.
I want to earn a good salary.
She is going to move to the city.
I am going to change my habits.

quiz.question: 'Going to' is used for what?
quiz.explanation: Going to expresses planned future actions.

quiz.question: What is Bat going to do this summer?
quiz.explanation: Bat is going to study English and visit his uncle.

quiz.question: Which cities are they going to visit?
quiz.explanation: They are going to visit Seoul and Busan.

quiz.question: What food are they going to eat?
quiz.explanation: They are going to eat Korean food.

quiz.question: When are Bat and Sarnai going to South Korea?

page1.grammarTopic: Going to — plans and intentions for the future
page1.grammarExplanation: 'Going to' is used to talk about PLANS and INTENTIONS — things you have already decided to do in the future. Form: am/is/are + going to + BASE VERB. 'I am going to study tomorrow.' For negatives: am/is/are + NOT + going to + BASE VERB. For questions: am/is/are + subject + going to + BASE VERB? 'Going to' нь ирээдүйд хийхээр ШИЙДСЭН ЗҮЙЛИЙН ТӨЛӨВЛӨГӨӨ, санаануудын тухай ярихад хэрэглэгддэг. Хэлбэр: am/is/are + going to + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ. Үгүйсгэл: am/is/are + NOT + going to. Асуулт: am/is/are + субьект + going to + үндсэн үйлүг?
commonMistakes: I going to study → I AM going to study
She is going to studies → She is going to STUDY
I am go to travel → I am GOING TO travel
Are you going to traveled? → Are you going to TRAVEL?

page2.readingPassage: Bat has big plans for his future. Next year, he is going to graduate from school. After that, he is going to study computer science at university. His dream is to be a programmer. He is going to work for a big tech company. Sarnai is going to study art. She wants to be a famous artist. This summer, they both are going to travel to South Korea together. They are so excited about their future!

keyPhrases.note: I am + going to + base verb
She is + going to + base verb

keyPhrases.english: I am not going to be late
keyPhrases.note: am not + going to + base verb

keyPhrases.english: What are you going to do?
keyPhrases.mongolian: Та юу хийх гэж байна вэ?
keyPhrases.note: What + are + you + going to + do?

keyPhrases.english: Next year I am going to...

speakingQuestions: What are you going to do this weekend? / Та энэ долоо хоногийн амралтаар юу хийх гэж байна?
What are you going to study next year?
Are you going to travel this summer?
What job are you going to have in the future?
What are you going to eat for dinner tonight?
Are you going to practice English today?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What are you going to do this weekend?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I am going to visit my grandparents.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Are you going to stay there long?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes, I am going to stay for two days.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: That sounds wonderful! What about next year?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Next year I am going to start university.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: What are you going to study?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I am going to study English and business.

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Today we talk about future plans. What are you going to do this summer? Bat? Bat: I am going to study English every day and visit my uncle in the countryside. Teacher: Great! Are you going to travel anywhere? Bat: Yes! Sarnai and I are going to go to South Korea next year! Sarnai: Yes! We are going to visit Seoul and Busan. I am so excited! Teacher: Wonderful! What are you going to do there? Sarnai: We are going to see K-pop concerts, eat Korean food, and visit museums. Teacher: That sounds amazing! English will really help you there!

listeningQuestions.question: What is Bat going to do this summer?
When are Bat and Sarnai going to South Korea?
Which cities are they going to visit?
What are they going to see?
What food are they going to eat?
What else are they going to visit?
What does the teacher say English will help with?

homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences about YOUR plans using 'I am going to...'
Vocabulary: Write your dream job and 3 things you are going to do to achieve it.
Speaking: Tell someone: 'I am going to... this weekend / next year.'
Review: am/is/are + going to + BASE verb · 'gonna' is only spoken — never write it!
